title: Jewish Communities in Southeast Asia: Singapore, Bangkok, and Beyond
title_zh: 东南亚的犹太社区:新加坡、曼谷及更远的地方
title_he: קהילות יהודיות בדרום מזרח אסיה: סינגפור, בנגקוק ומעבר
description: A practical guide to Jewish life in Singapore (Maghain Aboth Synagogue), Bangkok (Beth Elisheva), and other Southeast Asian cities.
chinese_parallels: Late Qing Dynasty trade networks (清朝晚期)
Parallel Chinese merchant communities in Southeast Asia

historical_periods.jewish: 19th-21st century Jewish migration to Southeast Asia
historical_periods.chinese_equivalent: Late Qing Dynasty (清朝晚期, late 19th century)
historical_periods.description: Era of global trade expansion and colonial influence in Asia

target_audience: Chinese learners interested in Jewish diaspora communities, travelers to Southeast Asia, Jews planning to visit or relocate to the region
learning_outcomes: Understand the history and current state of Jewish communities in Singapore and Bangkok
Learn about practical resources for observant travelers (synagogues, kosher food, mikvaot)
Recognize the pattern of Jewish diaspora community-building across history
Discover the Sephardic traditions that characterize Southeast Asian synagogues
Appreciate the challenges and opportunities of small Jewish communities in diverse cultural settings
author: Torah Light Writing Team
